compiled_state.title: Implement format_trailer_flags in apply-attribution-trailers.sh
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: Implement format_trailer_flags() in plugins/dso/scripts/apply-attribution-trailers.sh. Reads attribution-contributors.jsonl (via read_and_deduplicate output), emits one --trailer 'DSO-Agent: <value>' line per unique subagent_type, one --trailer 'DSO-Skill: <value>' per unique skill_name, one --trailer 'DSO-Model: <value>' per unique model. Scalar trailers are a placeholder (T4-impl fills them). AC: (1) 2 distinct subagent_type values -> exactly 2 '--trailer DSO-Agent:' lines (use grep -c '^--trailer' not wc -c); (2) empty JSONL -> no output (use test -z or grep -c . equals 0); (3) skill entry -> DSO-Skill flag present; (4) all T3b-test tests pass; (5) validate.sh --ci passes.

comments.body:
Gap Analysis AC amendment: Use read-config.sh (external script), not read_config_value (nonexistent function).

apply-attribution-trailers.sh is a standalone script, not a function in post-functions.sh, so it cannot rely on functions sourced by dispatcher.sh. read_config_value does not exist as a standalone function in this codebase.

Amended implementation pattern:
  - Resolve _PLUGIN_ROOT via BASH_SOURCE / readlink at top of script (standard plugin pattern)
  - Call: config_val=$(bash "$_PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/read-config.sh" attribution.enabled 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  - If config_val != 'true', exit 0

This matches the pattern used throughout the codebase (e.g., read-config.sh is called similarly in pre-bash-functions.sh and session-misc-functions.sh).
comments.author: Test
